description_long: This rustic cabin features a log and timber construction with a prominent gable roof, covered in thatch or weathered wood. The front facade includes a small covered porch with wooden railings and steps leading up from a stone path, flanked by barrels and crates. The walls are a mix of exposed logs and plaster, with a stone foundation visible at the base. Windows are modest in size and asymmetrically placed, with one on each long side and none on the gable ends. The roof is supported by visible timber beams and features crossed wooden elements at the ridge. The overall appearance suggests a traditional, hand-built structure suited for rural or historical settings.
description: A rustic log-and-timber cabin with a gable thatched roof, small porch, and stone foundation. Asymmetrical windows and weathered wood details evoke a traditional, hand-built style.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main entrance with a small covered porch, wooden steps, and barrels beside the door.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the long thatched roof, a single window, and the stone foundation along the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a simple wooden door centered on the gable end, with no porch or windows.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows another window and the continuation of the thatched roof and stone base.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with exposed timber beams and thatched or weathered wood covering, featuring crossed ridge elements
attribute_window_arrangement: Two medium-sized rectangular windows, one on each long side of the cabin, asymmetrically placed and not grouped; no windows on the gable ends
attribute_door_placement: One main door centered on the front facade beneath the porch, and one rear door centered on the back gable end
